policy_statement: Publish and implement Aotearoa New Zealand's first national adaptation plan (2022–28) as a government-led multi-year work programme prepared under the Climate Change Response Act 2002.
verbatim_text:
This national adaptation plan is published by the Minister of Climate Change under section 5ZT of the Climate Change Response Act 2002.

This plan is a Government-led plan for all New Zealanders that brings together existing actions and proposed future work into a multi-year work programme. Together, these set out what central government will do over the next six years to enable all levels of government, sectors and communities and all New Zealanders to better understand the risks and take action to address them.
section_header: Our first national adaptation plan 2022-28 / A Government-led plan for all New Zealanders
extraction_rationale: Formal government plan published under legislation with a defined timeframe (2022–28) and a stated programme of actions.

policy_statement: Oversee implementation of the emissions reduction plan and national adaptation plan through a climate change interdepartmental executive board and the Climate Change Response Ministers Group, with biennial reporting by the Climate Change Commission.
verbatim_text:
A climate change interdepartmental executive board will oversee the emissions reduction plan and national adaptation plan, and report on progress. The Climate Change Response Ministers Group, chaired by the Prime Minister, will oversee the plan and drive progress.

Every two years, He Pou a Rangi - Climate Change Commission will report to the Minister of Climate Change on the implementation and effectiveness of the national adaptation plan.
section_header: Implementing the plan and making sure it is effective
extraction_rationale: Creates specific governance and reporting arrangements, including a two-yearly reporting commitment.

policy_statement: Action 3.1: Provide access to the latest climate projections data by producing national climate projection datasets for Aotearoa by January 2023.
verbatim_text:
## Action 3.1: Provide access to the latest climate projections data

The National Institute of Water and Atmospheric Research (NIWA) Projections Project is working to make the global climate projections from the most recent Intergovernmental Panel on Climate Change (IPCC) report (AR6 WG1) more applicable to Aotearoa.

By January 2023, national climate projection datasets for Aotearoa are being produced.
section_header: Action 3.1: Provide access to the latest climate projections data
extraction_rationale: Named government action with a clear deliverable and deadline (national datasets by January 2023).

policy_statement: Action 3.3: Establish a platform for Māori climate action, including an interim ministerial advisory committee by end of 2022 and development of an enduring platform by 2024, with funding for kaupapa Māori, tangata Māori climate actions.
verbatim_text:
## Action 3.3: Establish a platform for Māori climate action

The platform will build on three focus areas: ... activate kaupapa Māori, tangata Māori solutions - to enable community action, kaupapa Māori, tangata Māori actions and solutions for the climate emergency will be funded.

By the end of 2022, the Interim Ministerial Committee will be established. By 2024, phase two of the platform for Māori climate action will be developed.
section_header: Action 3.3: Establish a platform for Māori climate action
extraction_rationale: Specific institutional mechanism with stated phases, deadlines, and resourcing/funding intent.

policy_statement: Action 3.4: Raise awareness of climate hazards and preparedness through public education campaigns, including development of a public education strategy for natural hazards by end of March 2024.
verbatim_text:
## Action 3.4: Raise awareness of climate hazards and how to prepare

Enable and work with communities... to make emergency preparedness a part of everyday life through public awareness and advice. Examples are public education campaigns and resources...

By the end of March 2024, a public education strategy will be developed for natural hazards, and there will be increased availability of information on preparedness for extreme weather events.
section_header: Action 3.4: Raise awareness of climate hazards and how to prepare
extraction_rationale: Clear government-led public education commitment with a timeline and defined output (strategy).

policy_statement: Action 3.5: Implement mandatory climate-related disclosures for about 200 of New Zealand's largest financial market participants and decide by 2024 whether to extend mandatory disclosures to public entities.
verbatim_text:
## Action 3.5: Support high-quality implementation of climate-related disclosures and explore expansion

The Financial Sector (Climate-related Disclosures and Other Matters) Amendment Act 2021 requires about 200 of Aotearoa New Zealand's largest financial market participants to analyse and publicly disclose their climate-related risks and opportunities each year.

The Government will decide whether to extend mandatory disclosure requirements to public entities by 2024.
section_header: Action 3.5: Support high-quality implementation of climate-related disclosures and explore expansion
extraction_rationale: Binding mechanism (Amendment Act requirement) with quantified coverage and a specified future government decision point.

policy_statement: Action 3.6: Improve natural hazard information on Land Information Memoranda (LIMs) by changing legal requirements, with likely legislative changes to the Local Government Official Information and Meetings Act 1987 by end of 2023.
verbatim_text:
## Action 3.6: Improve natural hazard information on Land Information Memoranda

Changes to legal requirements for Land Information Memoranda (LIMs) will help people make better-informed decisions about natural hazard risk when buying a property.

By end of 2023, legislative changes will likely have been made to the Local Government Official Information and Meetings Act 1987 to provide for improved natural hazard disclosure in LIMs.
section_header: Action 3.6: Improve natural hazard information on Land Information Memoranda
extraction_rationale: Explicit commitment to legal change with a deadline and purpose tied to hazard disclosure.

policy_statement: Action 3.7: Deliver a rolling programme of targeted, non-statutory adaptation guidance for decision makers to assess and manage climate risks.
verbatim_text:
## Action 3.7: Deliver a rolling programme of targeted adaptation guidance

The Government will roll out a programme of non-statutory guidance for decision makers to assess and manage climate risks. This could also support regulatory requirements to be developed through the National Planning Framework.
section_header: Action 3.7: Deliver a rolling programme of targeted adaptation guidance
extraction_rationale: Umbrella programme explicitly introducing multiple specific guidance products listed as sub-items (3.7.1–3.7.8).

policy_statement: Promote the use of updated Aotearoa New Zealand Climate Change Projections guidance highlighting changes from AR6 to support updated risk management plans.
verbatim_text:
## 3.7.1: Promote the use of the Aotearoa New Zealand Climate Change Projections guidance

An update for Aotearoa that highlights changes from the 2021 AR6 IPCC.12 report. The update will enable stakeholders to quickly assess where they may need to adapt their risk management plans to account for the latest science.
parent_policy_name: Action 3.7: Deliver a rolling programme of targeted, non-statutory adaptation guidance for decision makers to assess and manage climate risks.
section_header: 3.7.1: Promote the use of the Aotearoa New Zealand Climate Change Projections guidance
extraction_rationale: Specific guidance deliverable under the rolling programme.

policy_statement: Produce adaptation guidance for central government policy makers, including a methodology to ensure adaptation is considered in new policy and services to avoid increased exposure or vulnerability.
verbatim_text:
## 3.7.2: Produce adaptation guidance for central government policy makers

A methodology for central government to ensure it considers adaptation in new policy and services. This will avoid increased exposure or vulnerability to climate impacts.
parent_policy_name: Action 3.7: Deliver a rolling programme of targeted, non-statutory adaptation guidance for decision makers to assess and manage climate risks.
section_header: 3.7.2: Produce adaptation guidance for central government policy makers
extraction_rationale: Specific methodology commitment under the guidance programme.

policy_statement: Produce guidance for dynamic adaptive pathways planning (DAPP) for central and local government to plan adaptation under uncertain climate futures.
verbatim_text:
## 3.7.3: Produce guidance for dynamic adaptive pathways planning (DAPP)

A guide for central and local government on how to plan for adaptation in a context of uncertain climate futures.
parent_policy_name: Action 3.7: Deliver a rolling programme of targeted, non-statutory adaptation guidance for decision makers to assess and manage climate risks.
section_header: 3.7.3: Produce guidance for dynamic adaptive pathways planning (DAPP)
extraction_rationale: Specific guidance product with a defined purpose.

policy_statement: Produce guidance on using different socio-economic scenarios for adaptation planning to help governments and businesses assess climate risks and plan responses.
verbatim_text:
## 3.7.4: Produce guidance on using different socio-economic scenarios for adaptation planning

Assists central and local government and businesses to consider future socio-economic scenarios when assessing climate risks, and planning how to manage them.
parent_policy_name: Action 3.7: Deliver a rolling programme of targeted, non-statutory adaptation guidance for decision makers to assess and manage climate risks.
section_header: 3.7.4: Produce guidance on using different socio-economic scenarios for adaptation planning
extraction_rationale: Concrete guidance output under an identified action sub-item.

policy_statement: Regularly update adaptation guidance for local government to support adaptation in planning and decisions, including communicating technical information and making climate decisions.
verbatim_text:
## 3.7.5: Regularly update adaptation guidance for local government

Supports local government to consider adaptation in planning and decisions. This will include guidance on communicating scientific and technical information to communities, and on making climate decisions.
parent_policy_name: Action 3.7: Deliver a rolling programme of targeted, non-statutory adaptation guidance for decision makers to assess and manage climate risks.
section_header: 3.7.5: Regularly update adaptation guidance for local government
extraction_rationale: Ongoing guidance update commitment for a specific user group (local government).

policy_statement: Produce guidance on integrating mātauranga Māori into adaptive planning and working with mana whenua.
verbatim_text:
## 3.7.6: Produce guidance on integrating mātauranga Māori into adaptive planning and working with mana whenua

Helps central and local government effectively engage with hapū, iwi and Māori when managing risk and planning for adaptation.
parent_policy_name: Action 3.7: Deliver a rolling programme of targeted, non-statutory adaptation guidance for decision makers to assess and manage climate risks.
section_header: 3.7.6: Produce guidance on integrating mātauranga Māori into adaptive planning and working with mana whenua
extraction_rationale: Specific guidance deliverable focused on Māori knowledge and engagement.

policy_statement: Produce guidance for preparing adaptation plans using a standard approach under uncertain climate projections.
verbatim_text:
## 3.7.7: Produce guidance for preparing adaptation plans

Enables communities and different audiences, sectors and levels of government to produce their own adaptation plans, using a standard approach under conditions of uncertainty in climate projections.
parent_policy_name: Action 3.7: Deliver a rolling programme of targeted, non-statutory adaptation guidance for decision makers to assess and manage climate risks.
section_header: 3.7.7: Produce guidance for preparing adaptation plans
extraction_rationale: Concrete guidance product intended to standardise adaptation planning.

policy_statement: Regularly update the guide to local climate change risk assessments on a six-yearly cycle after each national risk assessment.
verbatim_text:
## 3.7.8: Regularly update the guide to local climate change risk assessments

Sets out a step-by-step process for local risk assessments. It will be updated on a six-yearly cycle after the release of each national climate change risk assessment.
parent_policy_name: Action 3.7: Deliver a rolling programme of targeted, non-statutory adaptation guidance for decision makers to assess and manage climate risks.
section_header: 3.7.8: Regularly update the guide to local climate change risk assessments
extraction_rationale: Specific commitment with a defined update cycle linked to risk assessments.

policy_statement: Action 3.8: Develop and publish guidance for infrastructure asset owners to assess climate risks to physical assets and services, with publication by 2026.
verbatim_text:
## Action 3.8: Develop guidance for assessing risk and impact on physical assets and the services they provide

Te Waihanga will scope and deliver guidance for asset owners to evaluate risks to their physical assets and the services they provide.

By 2026, the guidance will be published.
section_header: Action 3.8: Develop guidance for assessing risk and impact on physical assets and the services they provide
extraction_rationale: Named guidance action with a clear deliverable and deadline (publish by 2026).

policy_statement: Action 3.10: Assess socioeconomic and climate vulnerability for Māori by collecting and analysing social data against climate data to target resilience support.
verbatim_text:
## Action 3.10: Assess socioeconomic and climate vulnerability for Māori

This action will collect and analyse social data against climate data to determine where support for resilience is most needed.
section_header: Action 3.10: Assess socioeconomic and climate vulnerability for Māori
extraction_rationale: Specific intervention to produce an assessment to guide targeted support.

policy_statement: Action 3.11: Implement the National Disaster Resilience Strategy by designing a pathway to give effect to its vision, goals and objectives.
verbatim_text:
## Action 3.11: Implement the National Disaster Resilience Strategy

The National Emergency Management Agency (NEMA) will design a pathway to give effect to the National Disaster Resilience Strategy's vision, goals and objectives.
section_header: Action 3.11: Implement the National Disaster Resilience Strategy
extraction_rationale: Commitment to implement an existing national strategy via a defined pathway.

policy_statement: Action 3.12: Improve use of science, data, mātauranga Māori and local knowledge in emergency management by applying NEMA's 2021 Science Strategy framework.
verbatim_text:
## Action 3.12: Improve how science, data and knowledge are used to inform emergency management

The National Emergency Management Agency 's 2021 Science Strategy will be used to create a framework for scientific engagement, promotion and influence for risk reduction, readiness, response and recovery.
section_header: Action 3.12: Improve how science, data and knowledge are used to inform emergency management
extraction_rationale: Specific planned framework to guide emergency management decisions.

policy_statement: Action 4.1: Reform the resource management system by repealing and replacing the Resource Management Act 1991 with new Acts, expected to be passed in 2023, to better enable climate adaptation and manage natural hazard risks including retreat.
verbatim_text:
## Action 4.1: Reform the resource management system

In early 2021, the Government announced its intention to repeal the Resource Management Act 1991 and replace it with a Natural and Built Environments Act, a Spatial Planning Act and a Climate Adaptation Act.

In 2023, the Natural and Built Environments Act and the Spatial Planning Act are expected to be passed.
section_header: Action 4.1: Reform the resource management system
extraction_rationale: Major legislative reform programme with specified replacement Acts and an expected passage date.

policy_statement: Action 4.2: Set national direction on natural hazard risk management and climate adaptation through the National Planning Framework, including hazard risk assessment methodologies; release a draft NPF for consultation after the Natural and Built Environments Act is passed.
verbatim_text:
## Action 4.2: Set national direction on natural hazard risk management and climate adaptation through the National Planning Framework

The first iteration of the National Planning Framework will contain hazard risk assessment methodologies and direction.

The draft National Planning Framework will be released for consultation shortly after the Natural and Built Environments Act is passed.
section_header: Action 4.2: Set national direction on natural hazard risk management and climate adaptation through the National Planning Framework
extraction_rationale: Clear commitment to a national framework and consultation timing tied to legislation.

policy_statement: Action 4.3: Establish an initiative for resilient public housing by developing and using a decision-making framework by 2024 to determine adaptation actions for new and existing public housing assets.
verbatim_text:
## Action 4.3: Establish an initiative for resilient public housing

A framework for public housing assets will be developed to determine the actions to adapt new and existing assets.

By 2024, a decision-making framework will be in use to determine adaptation actions for both new and existing assets.
section_header: Action 4.3: Establish an initiative for resilient public housing
extraction_rationale: Named initiative with a concrete tool (framework) and deadline for use (by 2024).

policy_statement: Action 4.4: Update housing and urban development funding programmes (including Māori housing) to consider climate hazard costs, and begin reviewing/amending programmes by 2024.
verbatim_text:
## Action 4.4: Embed adaptation in funding models for housing and urban development, including Māori housing

Existing funding programmes for urban development and housing, including Māori housing, will be updated to consider the costs of existing and future climate hazards.

By 2024, the Ministry of Housing and Urban Development (HUD) will have begun reviewing funding programmes it administers and amending them to appropriately consider climate-related risk.
section_header: Action 4.4: Embed adaptation in funding models for housing and urban development, including Māori housing
extraction_rationale: Specific intervention to change funding programme criteria with a timeline.

policy_statement: Action 4.5: Create new water services entities via legislation by 2024 to improve water services and embed climate resilience objectives; make entities operational from 1 July 2024.
verbatim_text:
## Action 4.5: Reform institutional arrangements for water services

The Government will create new water entities that will work with councils and communities to improve health and wellbeing outcomes for our communities and protect our environment for generations to come.

By 2024, legislation to establish water services entities will be enacted (to enable them to become operational from 1 July 2024).
section_header: Action 4.5: Reform institutional arrangements for water services
extraction_rationale: Legislative/institutional reform with explicit timing and operational start date.

policy_statement: Action 4.6: Integrate climate risk and adaptation into Treasury investment guidance for infrastructure, completing advice and associated decisions by 2024.
verbatim_text:
## Action 4.6: Integrate adaptation into Treasury decisions on infrastructure

The Treasury... will integrate consideration of climate risks and future adaptation into this guidance.

By 2024, advice on the best way forward, and any associated budget bid or Cabinet decisions, will be complete and future implementation measures defined.
section_header: Action 4.6: Integrate adaptation into Treasury decisions on infrastructure
extraction_rationale: Specific commitment to change central government investment guidance with a deadline.

policy_statement: Action 4.7: Integrate adaptation into Waka Kotahi's investment decision-making and the National Land Transport Programme by 2024.
verbatim_text:
## Action 4.7: Integrate adaptation into Waka Kotahi decision-making

Waka Kotahi will incorporate adaptation in the Investment Decision Making Framework...

By 2024, Waka Kotahi will integrate adaptation into planning, investment and decision-making for the National Land Transport Programme.
section_header: Action 4.7: Integrate adaptation into Waka Kotahi decision-making
extraction_rationale: Clear integration commitment with a specified implementation deadline (by 2024).

policy_statement: Action 5.1: Develop and introduce a Climate Adaptation Bill establishing a managed retreat framework, expected to be introduced by end of 2023.
verbatim_text:
Action 5.1: Pass legislation to support managed retreat

The Government will develop legislation to address the complex technical, legal and financial issues associated with managed retreat...

By the end of 2023, the Government is expecting to introduce the Climate Adaptation Bill, setting out the managed retreat framework.
section_header: Action 5.1: Pass legislation to support managed retreat
extraction_rationale: Legislative commitment with a stated bill and introduction deadline.

policy_statement: Action 5.4: Develop options to support access and affordability of home flood insurance, with government advice and decisions on next steps by end of 2022.
verbatim_text:
## Action 5.4: Develop options for home flood insurance

This work includes exploring options to support access and affordability of flood insurance. The Government intends to develop options that ensure home flood insurance continues to play an appropriate role in supporting community resilience.

By the end of 2022, the Government will have received advice on flood insurance options and agreed to next steps.
section_header: Action 5.4: Develop options for home flood insurance
extraction_rationale: Defined policy development commitment with a clear deadline for advice and next steps.

policy_statement: Action 5.7: Reduce and manage climate hazard impacts on homes and buildings through research, property-level guidance, an assessment framework, and investigation of incentives; generate sufficient data by August 2024 to inform assessment framework development and regulatory updates.
verbatim_text:
## Action 5.7: Reduce and manage the impacts of climate hazards on homes and buildings

This action includes five components... develop an assessment framework... investigate incentives...

By August 2024, enough data will be available to inform the development of an assessment framework in years 3-4 (2024-26) and regulatory updates.
section_header: Action 5.7: Reduce and manage the impacts of climate hazards on homes and buildings
extraction_rationale: Concrete multi-component programme explicitly intended to inform regulatory change, with a stated near-term data milestone.

policy_statement: Action 5.9: Prioritise nature-based solutions in planning and regulations for climate adaptation and carbon removals, with a framework developed by 2024 and implementation underway.
verbatim_text:
## Action 5.9: Prioritise nature-based solutions

To address the climate and biodiversity crises together, the Government will prioritise nature-based solutions in planning and regulations, where possible, for both carbon removals and climate change adaptation.

By 2024, a framework will be developed, and prioritising of nature-based solutions in regulations and planning will be underway and future implementation measures confirmed.
section_header: Action 5.9: Prioritise nature-based solutions
extraction_rationale: Specific government commitment with an implementation framework and deadline.

policy_statement: Action 6.1: Implement DOC's Climate Change Adaptation Action Plan (CCAAP) and establish a reporting framework by 2024 to report DOC adaptation progress.
verbatim_text:
## Action 6.1: Implement the Department of Conservation Climate Change Adaptation Action Plan

The plan aims to assess ecosystem and species vulnerability to climate impacts, and the adaptation actions to enhance their resilience.

By 2024, a reporting framework on the implementation of the CCAAP will be in place. DOC adaptation work progress will be reported against the framework.
section_header: Action 6.1: Implement the Department of Conservation Climate Change Adaptation Action Plan
extraction_rationale: Implementation and monitoring/reporting commitment with a specific deadline.

policy_statement: Action 6.3: Implement Te Mana o te Taiao – Aotearoa New Zealand Biodiversity Strategy 2020, establishing collaborative structures by 2024 and launching the second implementation plan.
verbatim_text:
## Action 6.3: Implement Te Mana o te Taiao - Aotearoa New Zealand Biodiversity Strategy 2020

Te Mana o te Taiao... guides all those who work with, or have an impact on, biodiversity...

By 2024, the collaborative structures and channels into the biodiversity sector will be established and the second implementation plan will be launched.
section_header: Action 6.3: Implement Te Mana o te Taiao - Aotearoa New Zealand Biodiversity Strategy 2020
extraction_rationale: Named strategy implementation with concrete milestones and timeline.

policy_statement: Action 6.4: Bring the National Policy Statement on Indigenous Biodiversity (NPS-IB) into force by 2024 and begin implementation to protect, maintain, and restore indigenous biodiversity.
verbatim_text:
## Action 6.4: Implement the proposed National Policy Statement on Indigenous Biodiversity

The objective of the NPS-IB is to protect, maintain and restore indigenous biodiversity.

By 2024, the NPS-IB will be in force and implementation will have begun.
section_header: Action 6.4: Implement the proposed National Policy Statement on Indigenous Biodiversity
extraction_rationale: Regulatory instrument with a stated in-force date and implementation start.

policy_statement: Action 6.6: Implement the Water Availability and Security programme to help food and fibre businesses and rural communities adapt to variable water availability, including forming a permanent MPI team by 2024.
verbatim_text:
## Action 6.6: Implement the Water Availability and Security programme

The Water Availability and Security programme will help food and fibre businesses and rural communities adapt to increasingly variable natural water availability...

By 2024, the Ministry for Primary Industries (MPI) will form a permanent team to address water availability and security in the food and fibre sectors and rural communities.
section_header: Action 6.6: Implement the Water Availability and Security programme
extraction_rationale: Defined programme with organisational resourcing milestone (permanent team by 2024).

policy_statement: Action 6.7: Implement the National Policy Statement on Freshwater Management 2020, with regional councils notifying plans implementing it by 2024.
verbatim_text:
## Action 6.7: Implement the National Policy Statement on Freshwater Management 2020

The National Policy Statement on Freshwater Management 2020 (NPS-FM 2020)... requires councils to give effect to Te Mana o te Wai...

By 2024, regional councils will notify plans implementing the NPS-FM 2020.
section_header: Action 6.7: Implement the National Policy Statement on Freshwater Management 2020
extraction_rationale: Regulatory implementation commitment with a clear deadline for regional plan notification.

policy_statement: Action 7.4 (proposed): Update Building Code performance requirements and related guidance/tools using forward-looking climate data to ensure buildings can withstand more extreme climate hazards.
verbatim_text:
## Action 7.4: Update regulatory requirements to ensure buildings are designed and constructed to withstand more extreme climate hazards

Updated forward-looking climate data and modelling... will be used to:
- update Building Code performance requirements
- identify and add climate hazards not currently in the Building Code
- produce guidance and tools to help people meet new performance requirements.
section_header: Action 7.4: Update regulatory requirements to ensure buildings are designed and constructed to withstand more extreme climate hazards
extraction_rationale: Specific planned regulatory update with clear mechanisms (Building Code updates and new hazards).

policy_statement: Develop and implement the Waka Kotahi Climate Adaptation Plan (2022–28), including a reporting framework by 2024.
verbatim_text:
In 2022, Waka Kotahi will publish and begin applyin g an adaptation plan. This will outline how Waka Kotahi will adapt to climate change through the design, delivery, operation and use of the land transport system.

By 2024, a reporting framework on the implementation of the adaptation plan will be developed.
section_header: Action 8.1: Develop and implement the Waka Kotahi Climate Adaptation Plan
extraction_rationale: This is a named government action with clear commitments (publish and begin applying the plan in 2022; develop a reporting framework by 2024) and an explicit timeframe.

policy_statement: Develop the National Energy Strategy by the end of 2024, collaborating with Māori and engaging energy system stakeholders, signalling pathways away from fossil fuels.
verbatim_text: The Government has committed to developing an energy strategy by the end of 2024, fully collaborating and engaging with Māori and working with energy system stakeholders. The strategy addresses challenges in the energy sector and signals pathways away from fossil fuels.
section_header: Action 8.2: Develop the National Energy Strategy
extraction_rationale: Explicit government commitment with a deadline (end of 2024) and a specific intervention (develop an energy strategy).

policy_statement: Manage dry-year risk through the New Zealand Battery Project by assessing options (including pumped hydro at Lake Onslow) and completing a feasibility study by end of 2022.
verbatim_text: The New Zealand Battery Project will assess the options for managing dry-year risk in a highly renewable electricity system. The current focus is on the feasibility of pumped hydroelectricity at Lake Onslow. The feasibility study will be completed by the end of 2022.
section_header: Action 8.3: Manage dry-year risk through the New Zealand Battery Project
extraction_rationale: Named project with a specific deliverable and deadline (feasibility study completed by end of 2022).

policy_statement: In the Commerce Commission’s next reviews of regulated price-quality paths (from 2025 for electricity networks and from 2026 for gas networks), consider whether regulated network revenue limits should reflect increased prudent and efficient resilience costs to climate change.
verbatim_text: In the Commerce Commission's next reviews of regulated price-quality paths - from 2025 for electricity networks and from 2026 for gas networks - it will consider the extent to which revenue limits should provide for differe nt expenditure levels from the current period. This will include if expenditure levels need to change due to any increased costs of resilience to climate change, where these are based on robust forecasts.
section_header: Action 8.4: Provide for regulated network revenues to reflect the prudent and efficient costs of resilience
extraction_rationale: Regulatory commitment tied to specific future review dates and an explicit mechanism (revenue limits in regulated price-quality paths) to account for climate resilience costs.

policy_statement: Progress KiwiRail’s Rail Network Investment Programme (RNIP) as a 10-year investment programme to restore the rail network to a resilient and reliable state, with climate change mitigation as a key focus in resilience project investment decisions.
verbatim_text: KiwiRail's Rail Network Investment Programme (RNIP) is a 10-year programme of investment in Aotearoa New Zealand's rail network, to restore it to a resilient and reliable state. Mitigating climate change is a key focus within the RNIP when considering resilience projects for investment.
section_header: Action 8.5: Progress the Rail Network Investment Programme
extraction_rationale: Describes an ongoing, defined investment programme (RNIP) with a stated purpose and decision focus relevant to climate resilience.

policy_statement: Invest in public transport and active transport infrastructure (2022–28) to increase resilience of the transport system and reduce reliance on private vehicles.
verbatim_text: Investment in multi-modal infrastructure can increase the resilience of the transport system and help manage the vulnerability of existing assets. More use of public transport and active modes will help reduce reliance on private vehicles.
section_header: Action 8.6: Invest in public transport and active transport
extraction_rationale: A specific investment action with a defined intervention area (public and active transport) and timeframe.

policy_statement: Embed nature-based solutions in transport policy and investment (2022–25), including protecting existing carbon sinks and supporting new long-term carbon sequestration opportunities.
verbatim_text:
Key initiatives include:

- considering the role of nature-based solutions in reducing transport emissions and contributing to other benefits
- ensuring transport policy and investment encourage nature-based solutions, including protecting existing carbon sinks and supporting new long-term carbon sequestration opportunities.
section_header: Action 8.7: Embed nature-based solutions as part of the response to reducing transport emissions and improving climate adaptation and biodiversity outcomes
extraction_rationale: Clearly stated policy action with specific initiatives (policy/investment encouragement; protection of sinks; support sequestration).

policy_statement: Te Waihanga will convene a regular cross-sector event (2022–28) for asset owners to share information on implementing national adaptation plan actions and support alignment across the infrastructure sector.
verbatim_text: Te Waihanga will convene a regular event for local government, central government and private sector asset owners to share information on the implementation of actions in the national adaptation plan and to support alignment across the sector.
section_header: Action 8.8: Support knowledge sharing and the implementation of adaptation actions across the sector
extraction_rationale: A concrete institutional intervention (regular convening) to support implementation and alignment of adaptation actions.

policy_statement: Modernise the emergency management system including legislative reforms, clearer roles and responsibilities, work with communities and organisations, and strengthened partnership with Māori; adopt new legislation by 2024 and provide a national emergency management plan and improved guidance.
verbatim_text:
The National Emergency Management Agency (NEMA) plans to modernise emergency management, including through legislative reforms, working with communities and organisations, clearer roles and responsibilities, and a strengthened partnership with Māori.

By 2024, new emergency management legislation will be adopted, and a national emergency management plan and improved guidance will be provided across the emergency management system.
section_header: Action 9.1: Modernise the emergency management system
extraction_rationale: Specific planned reforms with a clear deadline (legislation by 2024) and concrete outputs (national plan and guidance).

policy_statement: Complete the Health National Adaptation Plan (HNAP) by end of 2022 and develop regional climate health action plans from 2023.
verbatim_text: By the end of 2022, the HNAP is expected to be completed. From 2023, regional climate health action plans will be developed.
section_header: Action 9.2: Develop the Health National Adaptation Plan
extraction_rationale: Named plan with explicit deliverables and timelines (completion by end of 2022; regional plans from 2023).

policy_statement: Expand the operational capacity and capability of the emergency management workforce at national, regional and local levels (2022–28).
verbatim_text: Work is ongoing to expand the operational capacity and capability of the emergency management workforce at the national, regional and local levels.
section_header: Action 9.3: Develop the emergency management workforce
extraction_rationale: Clear action to build workforce capacity/capability; although not quantified, it is a specific intervention with an assigned action and timeframe.

policy_statement: Implement the Climate Migration Action Plan (2022–24), including commissioning research on Pacific climate migration trends and using international development cooperation funds to support a Pacific regional approach to climate mobility.
verbatim_text: One of the key actions is commissioning research to improve understanding of Pacific climate migration trends and the impact on communities in the Pacific and Aotearoa. The other main actions focus on using international development cooperation funds for Pacific Island countries. This will support a Pacific regional approach to climate mobility, and advance Aotearoa and Pacific interests in the international context.
section_header: Action: 9.4: Implement the Climate Migration Action Plan
extraction_rationale: A named plan with specified implementation actions (commission research; allocate development cooperation funds) and timeframe.

policy_statement: Continue reforming the health and disability system (2022–25), including establishing a simpler, more coordinated system and strengthening population/public health with greater emphasis on equity and monitoring environmental threats.
verbatim_text:
The structure of the health and disability system and the delivery of health services are undergoing a period of reform. The new system will be simpler and more coordinated, allowing for better and more consistent care, shaped by the voices of consumers, communities and whānau.

The Public Health Agency within the Manatū Hauora Ministry of Health (MOH) will lead and strengthen population and public health, with greater emphasis on equity and the wider determinants of health such as income, education and housing. It will use data and other sources of intelligence to design policies and services that are better able to prevent disease and to monitor environmental threats to public health.
section_header: Action 9.5: Continue with the reform of the health and disability system
extraction_rationale: Ongoing government reform programme described with concrete institutional changes and functions relevant to climate-related health resilience.

policy_statement: Build community resilience through social cohesion by improving inclusion and participation to lessen instability and isolation caused by climate change (2023–27).
verbatim_text: This work will improve inclusion and participation in society and build community resilience to lessen instability and isolation caused by climate change.
section_header: Action 9.6: Build community resilience through social cohesion
extraction_rationale: Stated government work programme with a defined objective and intervention focus (social cohesion for resilience).

policy_statement: Strengthen teaching and learning related to climate change (2022–28) by supporting early learning services, schools and kura to include understanding of, and responses to, climate change in local curricula and marau ā-kura.
verbatim_text: It will support early learning services, schools and kura to include understanding of, and responses to, climate change in their local curricula and marau ā -kura (living, breathing curriculum), as well as learning that is important for social cohesion.
section_header: Action 9.7: Strengthen teaching and learning related to climate change
extraction_rationale: Specific education-system intervention (curriculum inclusion) under a named action with timeframe.

policy_statement: Continue to overhaul the welfare system (2022–28) through ongoing improvements in support and incomes to ensure adequate income/standard of living, dignity, and meaningful participation in communities.
verbatim_text: This action will make ongoing improvements in support - such as for employment, health and communities - and in incomes for those interacting with the welfare system. This aims to achieve the Government's vision of a welfare system that ensures people have an adequate income and standard of living; are treated with, and can live in, dignity; and can participate meaningfully in their communities.
section_header: Action 9.8: Continue to overhaul the welfare system
extraction_rationale: A defined government reform action with stated measures (improvements in support and incomes) and intended outcomes.

policy_statement: Seek to expand funding for Māori proactive community resilience by decentralising funding via an expanded Whānau Ora scope to plug funding gaps and support long-term resilience plans (2022–28).
verbatim_text:
This will seek to expand funding for Māori to build their community resilience through the COVID-19 pandemic, and plug funding gaps for communities to carry out their long-term resilience plans.

Funding would be decentralised by expanding the scope of funds provided through Whānau Ora.
section_header: Action 9.9: Expand current funding for proactive community resilience
extraction_rationale: Explicit intent to expand funding with a described delivery mechanism (decentralised via Whānau Ora); marked as proposed but still a concrete policy proposal.

policy_statement: Develop a proposal for a New Zealand income insurance scheme and, if introduced, legislate in 2022 and start operating the scheme in 2024/25 to provide up to seven months of income replacement for eligible job loss.
verbatim_text:
the Government is developing a proposal for a New Zealand income insurance scheme, which would support workers with a proportion of income replacement for up to seven months if they lose their job through no fault of their own due to a redundancy or a health condition or disability.

If a decision is made to introduce the proposed scheme, the Government would introduce legislation in 2022 and the scheme would start operating in 2024/25.
section_header: Action 9.10: Implement an income insurance scheme to support adaptive capacity of communities and the economy
extraction_rationale: Contains a specific policy instrument (income insurance) with operational design (up to seven months income replacement) and conditional but concrete legislative/implementation timeline.

policy_statement: Develop and deliver initiatives in 'Responding to the Climate Crisis: An Implementation Plan (2019)' for Defence, including mitigation and adaptation initiatives for Defence Force infrastructure, capability and response operations, and work with other agencies on system reforms (2022–28).
verbatim_text: The plan identifies actions Defence will take as part of a broader government programme on climate change and sustainability. It sets out initiatives for mitigation and adaptation of Defence Force infrastructure, capability and response operations that protect the security of New Zealanders and the region. Defence will also work with national security and emergency services sector agencies on system reforms.
section_header: Action 9.11: Develop and deliver initiatives in Responding to the Climate Crisis: An Implementation Plan (2019)
extraction_rationale: A proposed but specified defence-sector implementation plan with defined initiative areas (mitigation/adaptation, infrastructure/capability/operations) and interagency reform work.

policy_statement: Produce guidance and tools for monitoring and evaluating adaptation initiatives (2024/25), including tools such as real options analysis and online calculators to support adaptation decisions.
verbatim_text: This will help ensure adaptation actions effectively increase our resilience and manage the risks we face. Tool s such as real options analysis (ROA), online calculators and other assessment tools will help with adaptation decisions.
section_header: Action 9.12: Produce guidance and tools for monitoring and evaluating the impact of adaptation initiatives
extraction_rationale: A concrete deliverable (guidance and tools) with examples of tools, tied to a specified timeframe (Year 3 / 2024/25).

policy_statement: Expand existing and introduce new active labour market programmes (ALMPs) to support people into work or back to work and into new job opportunities arising from economic changes (2022–25).
verbatim_text: The Government has been reviewing the ALMPs it provides and funds. It has committed to expanding existing, and introducing new, ALMPs to support people into work, or back to work, and to take up new job opportunities arising from changes in the economy.
section_header: Action 9.13: Review of active labour market programmes
extraction_rationale: Explicit commitment to expand and introduce programmes (ALMPs), i.e., a specific intervention to build adaptive capacity in the labour market.

policy_statement: Strengthen fisheries rules by passing the Fisheries Amendment Bill by late 2022 to tighten rules on returns to port, enable more agile decisions via pre-set decision rules, and allow adjustments to catch limits and sustainability measures in response to changes in fish stock abundance.
verbatim_text:
The Government is progressing work on the Fisheries Amendment Bill , which includes several legislative changes to the Fisheries Act 1996 . The Bill sets tighter rules about what fish commercial fisheries must bring back to port and what they can legally return.

These rules will allow adjustment of the catch limits, and other sustainability measures within pre-agreed limits, in response to change in abundance.

By late 2022, the Fisheries Amendment Bill will be passed.
section_header: Action 10.2: Strengthen fisheries rules
extraction_rationale: Legislative reform with a specific deadline (late 2022) and explicit regulatory mechanisms affecting fisheries management.

policy_statement: Deliver the Aquaculture Strategy (2022–26 and ongoing), including releasing implementation plans by 2024 and reporting annually on environmental effects of aquaculture, with actions to support industry adaptation to climate change.
verbatim_text:
The Government's Aquaculture Strategy sets objectives and actions to support Aotearoa in becoming a world leader in sustainable and innovative aquaculture. One of the strategy's objectives is to support the industry to adapt to climate change.

By 2024, the Government will continue releasing implementation plans for the Aquaculture Strategy and reporting each year on the environmental effects of aquaculture.
section_header: Action 10.3: Deliver the Aquaculture Strategy
extraction_rationale: Named strategy with specified implementation and reporting commitments, including a clear milestone by 2024.

policy_statement: RBNZ will support financial system stability by introducing climate-related stress tests, incorporating climate change into supervisory engagements, and developing risk management guidance; integrate climate considerations into supervisory, stress-testing and policy work by March 2024.
verbatim_text:
The Reserve Bank of New Zealand (RBNZ) is taking action to help regulated financial entities identify and manage climate risks better . This includes:

- introducing climate-related stress tests that model the effect of severe but plausible scenarios on the balance sheets of regulated financial institutions
- incorporating climate change in scheduled supervisor engagements with the management and boards of regulated entities
- developing guidance on managing risk for the entities the RBNZ regulates.

By March 2024, the RBNZ will have climate change considerations increasingly integrated into its supervisory, stress-testing and policy work.
section_header: Action 10.4: Reserve Bank of New Zealand supports the stability of the financial system
extraction_rationale: Concrete regulatory/supervisory actions with a clear deadline (March 2024) and specific instruments (stress tests, guidance, supervision).

policy_statement: Deliver the Māori agribusiness extension programme for whenua Māori (2022–28) by extending the current pilot programme to provide resources and support to Māori landowning collectives and support intergenerational planning for climate change impacts.
verbatim_text:
This action will deliver a tailored extension programme for whenua Māori . This will support collectives of Māori landowners and agribusiness to take a te ao Māori approach to meeting their aspirations for their whenua in a productive and sustainable way.

It will extend the current pilot programme, which offers resources and support to Māori landowning collectives.
section_header: Action 10.5: Deliver the Māori agribusiness extension programme
extraction_rationale: Specific programme delivery and expansion (extend pilot) aimed at supporting adaptation planning for Māori landowners.

policy_statement: Continue delivering the Sustainable Land Management and Climate Change and Greenhouse Gas Inventory research programmes (2022–28) to fund research and develop tools to support agriculture adaptation, emissions measurement, and mitigation of land-use impacts on freshwater.
verbatim_text: This work will fund research and develop tools to support the agriculture sector to adapt to climate change better, measure emissions and mitigate land-use impacts on freshwater.
section_header: Action 10.6: Continue delivering the Sustainable Land Management and Climate Change and Greenhouse Gas Inventory research programmes
extraction_rationale: Ongoing funded research/tooling programme with explicit purposes relevant to adaptation and mitigation.

policy_statement: Continue delivering the Sustainable Food and Fibre Futures Fund (2022–28) to co-invest in innovative primary-sector projects that address risks such as climate change and deliver economic, environmental and social benefits.
verbatim_text: The Sustainable Food and Fibre (SFF) Futures Fund supports innovative projects that design and test new approaches and solutions to risks such as climate change in the primary sector. The fund supports innovation in Aotearoa New Zealand's food and fibre sector by co-investing in initiatives that bring economic, environmental and social benefits for all New Zealanders.
section_header: Action 10.7: Continue delivering the Sustainable Food and Fibre Futures Fund
extraction_rationale: A defined funding mechanism (co-investment fund) supporting climate-related innovation in the primary sector.

policy_statement: Establish innovation grants (2022–28) to boost private-sector R&D investment and support climate-focused innovation to enable adaptation and the transition to a low-emissions economy.
verbatim_text: These grants will boost investment in private sector research and development and support innovation. They will share the risk with Aotearoa companies. This means more climatefocused innovation should happen faster, to better enable adaptation action and the transition to a low-emissions economy.
section_header: Action 10.8: Establish innovation grants
extraction_rationale: A specific policy instrument (grants) aimed at accelerating climate-focused innovation with stated purpose.

policy_statement: Model the economic impacts of climate change on regional economies and provide guidance for assessing climate impacts in local economic decisions (2022–25) to support regional risk assessments, actions and investments.
verbatim_text: This work will involve modelling the economic impacts of a changing climate on regional economies and providing guidance for assessing climate impacts in local economic decisions.
section_header: Action 10.9: Identify the impacts of climate change on regional economies
extraction_rationale: Concrete analytical and guidance outputs (modelling and decision guidance) under a named action with timeframe.

policy_statement: Monitor residential insurance premiums and insurance uptake (2022–24) to better understand shifts in insurance availability and affordability.
verbatim_text: Monitoring residential premiums and uptake of insurance gives a better understanding of the scale of shifts in insurance availability and affordability.
section_header: Action 10.10: Monitor residential insurance premiums
extraction_rationale: Specific monitoring action intended to generate information on affordability/availability of insurance.

policy_statement: Include climate risk considerations in Treasury’s six-monthly economic and fiscal monitoring and forecasting (2022–28) by highlighting known climate adaptation risks in economic updates.
verbatim_text: Treasury's six -monthly economic updates are a core way to monitor and forecast the economic and fiscal environment. The updates highlight known quantifiable and unquantifiable climate adaptation risks as a tool in fiscal and economic management.
section_header: Action 10.11: Consider climate risk in economic and fiscal monitoring and forecasting
extraction_rationale: Defines an ongoing institutional practice change (integrating climate adaptation risks into official economic updates).

policy_statement: Implement the Government response to the Prime Minister’s Chief Science Advisor’s report on commercial fishing (2022–28 and ongoing), including actions to support innovation, progress an ecosystem approach to fisheries management, and protect significant habitats.
verbatim_text: The Government's response is underway and includes actions that support innovation across the system, progress an ecosystem approach to fisheries management and protect habitats of significance to fisheries management.
section_header: Action 10.12: Implement the Government response to the Prime Minister's Chief Science Advisor's report on commercial fishing
extraction_rationale: A defined implementation action of a government response, with specific action areas stated (innovation, ecosystem approach, habitat protection).

policy_statement: Deliver the Tourism Industry Transformation Plan (2022–2025) with regenerative tourism goals, using the Tourism Adaptation Roadmap as an input for the environmental phase to assess sector-wide risks and actions.
verbatim_text: The goal of this work is regenerative tourism. The first phase focuses on 'better work' and the second phase on the environment. The Tourism Adaptation Roadmap developed by the Aotearoa Circle will be an input for the environmental phase of the Tourism Industry Transformation Plan. This allows for a sector-wide assessment of risks and actions.
section_header: Action 10.14: Deliver the Tourism Industry Transformation Plan
extraction_rationale: Named plan with defined phases and a specified adaptation-related input to enable sector-wide assessment.

policy_statement: Review the settings for the International Visitor Conservation and Tourism Levy (IVL) (2022–24), including ensuring international visitors contribute to resilient, adaptable infrastructure and the natural environment they use.
verbatim_text: the Ministry of Business, Innovation and Employment (MBIE), directed by the Minister of Tourism, is reviewing the International Visitor Conservation and Tourism Levy (IVL). This includes ensuring international visitors contribute to resilient, adaptable infrastructure and the natural environment they use during their visit.
section_header: Action 10.15: Review the settings for the International Visitor Conservation and Tourism Levy
extraction_rationale: Specific review action of a levy with an explicit adaptation-related purpose for spending priorities.

policy_statement: Leverage government procurement for climate outcomes (2022/23) by checking whether the procurement policy framework enables mitigation and adaptation in government investments, particularly in construction contracts.
verbatim_text: This action w ill check the current government's procurement policy framework enables mitigation and adaptation in government investments. The greatest opportunity will likely be in construction contracts, making buildings and infrastructure resilient to a changing climate, and reducing carbon emissions and waste.
section_header: Action 10.16: Leverage government procurement for climate outcomes
extraction_rationale: A clear, time-bound action to assess/adjust procurement policy to enable mitigation and adaptation outcomes.

policy_statement: Support Māori small business resilience and transitions (2023–27) by enabling Māori SMEs to develop low-emissions growth strategies, respond to climate-related risks/opportunities, and adopt resilient ways of working, with participating businesses committing to reduce emissions or improve resilience.
verbatim_text: The aim of this work is to support Māori small and medium enterprises to develop low -emissions growth strategies, respond to climate-related risks and opportunities, and adopt resilient ways of working. Businesses would commit to reducing their emissions or improving their resilience to physical climate events.
section_header: Action 10.17: Support Māori small business resilience and transitions
extraction_rationale: A proposed but specific support programme with clear participant commitments (reduce emissions or improve resilience).

policy_statement: Research business adaptation preparedness and provide guidance for small businesses to adapt (2022–28), establishing a baseline and producing guidance/resources to reduce climate risks.
verbatim_text: This will help small businesses reduce their climate risks through targeted guidance and information. The guidance will be based on research that will increase understanding of what businesses are doing to adapt, and the pain points for business owners. Th e research will set a baseline for current action, inform future policy and drive the development of guidance and resources for small businesses.
section_header: Action 10.18: Research business adaptation preparedness and provide guidance for small businesses to adapt
extraction_rationale: Specific intervention combining research (baseline, understanding) and development of guidance/resources aimed at small business adaptation.

policy_statement: Enhance industry partnership networks (2022–28) to pool knowledge/resources and test, scale up and spread innovation to address complex issues such as climate adaptation.
verbatim_text: This action will pool knowledge and resources to solve sector problems. The partnerships will recognise the complexity and interrelatedness of issues, such as climate adaptation, and the need for sophisticated networks to test, scale up and spread innovation to help address challenges facing Aotearoa.
section_header: Action 10.19: Enhance industry partnership networks
extraction_rationale: A proposed but clearly described programme to create/strengthen partnership networks for innovation relevant to adaptation.

policy_statement: Establish central government oversight and coordination for implementing the national adaptation plan (2022–28), including annual agency reporting commencing in 2023.
verbatim_text:
This will provide transparency of implementation of the plan across government, improve coordination within central government and enable accountability.

Annual reporting requirements by agencies will be completed each year, commencing in 2023.
section_header: Action 11.1: Establish central government oversight and coordination for implementing the national adaptation plan
extraction_rationale: Concrete governance mechanism with a specified reporting requirement and start date (commencing 2023).
